title: RunContext (experiment)
description: Experiment design parameter half of the split RunContext contract, owned by experiment-attribution, per docs/architecture/data-ownership.md:39 and docs/architecture/contract-catalog.md:24.
$comment: compat_class: closed (user decision 2026-07-12, plan §1.2 item 4 'closed 승격 5건 일괄' — virtual-cooking-map.md; plan §5 acceptance matrix 'RunContext(=2파일...) | lifecycle open / experiment closed'). Authority: approved plan §1.1 R10 (RunContext split into 2 files, A안 confirmed — registry ties them back together via a shared catalog_name: 'RunContext' field, NOT authored in this schema file itself, out of scope for this unit); data-ownership.md:39 ('실험 설계 파라미터(engine, locale, observation cell) = experiment-attribution (사전등록 불변성 — 등록 시점 hash를 audit-ledger에 앵커링)'); contract-catalog.md:24 (idempotency key run_id, sensitivity customer-proprietary meta, retention 'run 후 재현성 장기; secret 참조 금지'). SECRETS REFERENCING FORBIDDEN (contract-catalog.md:24 'secret 참조 금지') — this object and its fields must never carry secret material or secret references; enforcement is a runtime Input Gate obligation, not schema-structurally provable from field shapes alone. The split JOIN KEY between this file and context/run-context-lifecycle/v1 is run_id (data-ownership.md:39).

run_id.$comment: tenancy-model.md:42 Identifier set — 'single FORGE run'. Join key back to context/run-context-lifecycle/v1 (data-ownership.md:39).

engine_id.$comment: data-ownership.md:39 lists 'engine' as one of the experiment design parameters owned by experiment-attribution. References the engine-id root type (closed enum, v1 = chatgpt-search only), not the engine_required_payload $def, since this field IS the engine_id value itself rather than a payload fragment requiring it.

locales.$comment: data-ownership.md:39 lists 'locale' as an experiment design parameter. A concrete BCP-47 pattern constraint is DEFERRED — narrowing the item type with a pattern later is a MAJOR-version change under this catalog's pattern-change-is-breaking rule (plan §1.1 R5), and this deferral/accepted-risk is recorded here explicitly rather than silently narrowed without citation.

observation_cell_ref.$comment: Opaque object-ref to the observation cell for this run's experiment design. The internal structure/fields of an observation cell belong to the QueryExperiment P1 contract family (docs/specs/SAENA_k3s_Package_and_Operations_Spec_v1.md §4.1), out of scope here — this field is intentionally just an opaque reference string, not an inline object.

registration_hash.$comment: Preregistration hash anchored to the audit-ledger at registration time (data-ownership.md:39 '사전등록 불변성 — 등록 시점 hash를 audit-ledger에 앵커링'; plan §1.2 item 3 lists H-3 evidence_ledger_hash(sha256) as the sibling precedent pattern for sha256-anchored hashes in this catalog). BYTE-BOUNDARY IMMUTABILITY RATIONALE for treating this as part of a CLOSED-class object: preregistration is a scientific/audit integrity mechanism — the hash's evidentiary value depends on it covering an EXACT, unambiguous byte serialization of the experiment design parameters at registration time. If this object were open (additionalProperties: true), a downstream producer could silently attach extra fields to a 'registered' experiment context after the fact without invalidating or being caught by the registration_hash check, undermining the pregistration guarantee the hash exists to provide. Closing the object's shape is therefore load-bearing for the audit property this contract exists to serve, not merely stylistic consistency with the rest of the catalog.
